rule: Underwriting must investigate any new application with flagged risk indicators, inconsistent information, or missing medical details.
Staff must request missing health declaration details from the member and notify underwriting if any disclosure is incomplete.
Claims staff must verify pre-existing conditions by checking application records and prior claims. If unclear, escalate to underwriting for assessment.
Approval exceptions for urgent treatments require clinical documentation, supervisor sign-off, and submission to underwriting for expedited review.
If underwriting does not respond within the defined SLA, staff must escalate the case to the underwriting team lead or escalation owner.
All escalations must be documented with case details, communication history, and supporting documents before submission to the escalation owner.
Escalation owners must acknowledge receipt of an escalation within one business day and provide a status update within two business days.
Staff must not confirm coverage or benefits to members until underwriting review is complete and written confirmation is received.

rule_name: Dental out of network
answer: Dental is treated as out of network if accessed via restricted Essential Partner clinics.

rule_name: Maternity waiting period basis
answer: Maternity waiting period is 45 days from the HDF sign date or policy inception, whichever is later.

rule_name: Dental out of network
answer: Dental is treated as out of network if accessed via restricted Essential Partner clinics.

rule_name: Direct billing does not override approval rules
answer: Direct billing status does not remove approval requirements, exclusions, sublimits, waiting periods, or medical necessity review. It only affects the billing pathway.
notes: Use when someone assumes direct billing equals guaranteed coverage.

rule_name: In-network hospital does not guarantee every doctor pathway
answer: Being in network at hospital level does not guarantee all doctors, departments, or specialty pathways are covered identically. Validate the relevant network route and benefit access rules.

rule_name: Verbal statements do not replace policy basis
answer: Coverage interpretation must rely on policy documents and approved internal guidance, not on informal or verbal statements alone.

rule_name: Emergency territory rule
answer: Emergency treatment outside the standard territory must still be assessed under the applicable territory and reimbursement rules. Do not approve all overseas treatment by default.

rule_name: Card usage does not guarantee claim payment
answer: Use of the member card does not guarantee payment. Claims remain subject to eligibility, exclusions, approvals, limits, documentation, and policy rules.

rule_name: Incomplete documents can stop claim assessment
answer: A claim or case cannot be finalized if mandatory supporting documents are missing. Assessment may remain pending until required documents are received and reviewed.
notes: Useful for pending or incomplete submission cases.

rule_name: Pre-approval failure can affect claim outcome
answer: If a service required pre-approval and that approval was not obtained, the claim must be reviewed under the applicable approval rule before any payment assumption is made.

rule_name: Provider statement does not replace claim completeness check
answer: A provider saying that everything was submitted does not replace internal completeness and quality checks. Coding, clinical notes, approvals, and attachments may still be incomplete.

rule_name: Network participation does not override benefit rules
answer: A provider being in network does not override exclusions, limits, approvals, medical necessity, or policy conditions. Network status and benefit eligibility are separate checks.
notes: Useful when users confuse network access with guaranteed payment.

rule_name: Escalate unclear rejection reasons before confirming
answer: If the rejection basis is unclear, escalate for technical or claims review before giving a definitive explanation to the client or provider.

rule_name: Psychology versus psychiatry distinction
answer: Psychology, psychiatry, and psychotherapy should not be merged into one assumption. Check the exact covered service description, visit logic, cap, and referral or approval rule.

rule_name: Policy wording ambiguity must be escalated
answer: If policy wording, schedule wording, or endorsements create uncertainty, escalate rather than giving a definitive answer from memory.

rule_name: Medical necessity still applies within covered benefit
answer: A benefit being listed does not automatically make every service payable. Medical necessity, approval requirements, exclusions, and utilization rules may still apply.

rule_name: Disclosure requirement
answer: Members must disclose all relevant medical history and conditions during application. Non-disclosure may affect coverage or claims.

rule_name: Missing documents can cause rejection
answer: If required documents are missing, the claim may be rejected or remain pending until all information is received.

rule_name: Provider follow-up does not guarantee closure
answer: A provider's follow-up does not guarantee claim closure. Internal review and documentation checks must still be completed.

rule_name: Unclear rejection reason must be escalated
answer: If the rejection reason is unclear, escalate to Claims Technical Review before providing a final explanation.
